bosses.name: Blackgaol Knight
bosses.lore: A lone black knight imprisoned in a gaol beneath the Gravesite Plain, wielding a greatsword with crushing precision. One of the first challenges in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC.

bosses.name: Chief Bloodfiend
bosses.lore: The chieftain of the bloodfiend tribe, a monstrous being of corrupted blood that serves as an early challenge in the Land of Shadow's outer reaches.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Swordmaster Onze
bosses.lore: A demi-human who has mastered swordsmanship, leading a pack of his kin in the Gravesite Plain's ruins with surprising speed and technique.

bosses.name: Divine Beast Dancing Lion
bosses.lore: A lion-like divine beast that channels storm and lightning, serving as a sacred guardian in the Land of Shadow and the gateway boss to the DLC.

bosses.name: Ghostflame Dragon
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon whose flame has turned cold and ghostly — a remnant of the Shadow Realm's forgotten age, breathing deathly spectral fire.

bosses.name: Rellana, Twin Moon Knight
bosses.lore: A knight of breathtaking speed who wields twin blades imbued with both the light of the moon and its shadow, a loyal servant of Messmer the Impaler.

bosses.name: Red Bear
bosses.lore: A massive bear of the Shadow Realm, territorial and ferocious, its fur matted with blood from countless battles in the Gravesite Plain.

bosses.name: Black Knight Edredd
bosses.lore: A fallen black knight of the Shadowlands wielding a heavy halberd, a veteran of wars fought long before the Tarnished arrived.

bosses.name: Black Knight Garrew
bosses.lore: One of the Shadow Realm's elite black knights, a master of mounted combat who patrols the upper heights of Scadu Altus.

bosses.name: Count Ymir, Mother of Fingers
bosses.lore: A noble of the Shadowlands whose body was reshaped by the fingers of an outer god until he became something beyond human — a vessel for divine influence.

bosses.name: Curseblade Labirith
bosses.lore: A curseblade warrior of the Shadow Realm who wields a blade suffused with fell energy, attacking with relentless and cursed aggression.

bosses.name: Death Knight
bosses.lore: An armored knight of death wielding a massive flail, stationed at key passages in the Land of Shadow as an unyielding guardian of the approaches.

bosses.name: Dryleaf Dane
bosses.lore: A wandering warrior who fights bare-handed using devastating dryleaf martial arts, his technique refined through countless battles across the Shadow Realm.

bosses.name: Ghostflame Dragon
bosses.lore: A ghostflame dragon haunting the plateau of Scadu Altus, its cold spectral breath capable of inflicting death blight on the unprepared.

bosses.name: Golden Hippopotamus
bosses.lore: A sacred golden beast of immense size that serves as guardian of an ancient sanctum in Scadu Altus, its hide nearly impervious to conventional weapons.

bosses.name: Messmer the Impaler
bosses.lore: Miquella's shadow and the keeper of the Elden Ring's darkest secret — a demigod of fire and serpents who burned the Land of Shadow on his mother's command.

bosses.name: Metyr, Mother of Fingers
bosses.lore: The primordial mother who spawned the fingers of the outer gods, a being of incomprehensible form residing at the heart of the Shadowlands.

bosses.name: Rakshasa
bosses.lore: A demon-like warrior of the Shadow Realm armored in blood-red plate, attacking with relentless aggression and supernatural speed that defies its size.

bosses.name: Jori, Elder Inquisitor
bosses.lore: An elder member of Messmer's inquisitors who hunted heretics across the Land of Shadow with fanatical devotion and brutal efficiency.

bosses.name: Ralva the Great Red Bear
bosses.lore: A massive red bear revered in the Shadow Realm, its strength legendary among the inhabitants of Scadu Altus.

bosses.name: Death Knight
bosses.lore: A death knight stationed at the base of the Rauh ruins, wielding a great flail and guarding the approach into the ancient structure.

bosses.name: Rugalea the Great Red Bear
bosses.lore: A great red bear that dwells at the base of the Rauh ruins, one of the enormous bears sacred to the inhabitants of the Shadow Realm.

bosses.name: Dancer of Ranah
bosses.lore: A spectral dancer of the cerulean coast who attacks with flowing, graceful movements that belie their lethal precision.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Queen Marigga
bosses.lore: A demi-human queen who rules over a tribe dwelling along the Cerulean Coast, wielding sorcery with surprising sophistication.

bosses.name: Ghostflame Dragon
bosses.lore: A ghostflame dragon that has made its lair along the Cerulean Coast, its deathly pale breath visible from across the shimmering waters.

bosses.name: Putrescent Knight
bosses.lore: A knight whose body has rotted away into something hollow and putrid yet still fighting, wielding a blade of frost and decay across the Cerulean Coast.

bosses.name: Death Rite Bird
bosses.lore: A monstrous corvid spirit of death rite performing rituals in Charo's Hidden Grave, a variant that wields ghostflame and deathly cold.

bosses.name: Lamenter
bosses.lore: A sorrowful being that dwells in the hidden grave, its mournful cries preceding devastating magical attacks born of grief and loss.

bosses.name: Tibia Mariner
bosses.lore: A skeletal boatman of death sailing through the Shadow Realm's hidden grave, summoning the undead as it navigates between worlds.

bosses.name: Ancient Dragon-Man
bosses.lore: A humanoid figure who has partially transformed into a dragon, dwelling among the crags of Jagged Peak, wielding lightning with draconic power.

bosses.name: Ancient Dragon Senessax
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon of immense age that has made its lair on the Jagged Peak, one of the elder drakes of the Shadow Realm.

bosses.name: Bayle the Dread
bosses.lore: The most fearsome dragon in the Shadow Realm — a monstrous beast of fire and lightning whose very name inspires terror. He drove Igon mad with his ferocity.

bosses.name: Jagged Peak Drake
bosses.lore: A pair of young drakes that inhabit the Jagged Peak, children of the ancient dragons who serve as the mountain's first line of defense.

bosses.name: Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame
bosses.lore: A lord who willingly surrendered himself to the frenzied flame, dwelling in the abyssal woods and commanding the yellow chaos that unmakes minds.

bosses.name: Commander Gaius
bosses.lore: A mounted commander of the Shadow Realm who charges on a massive boar, leading shadow soldiers with brutal tactical aggression across the Scaduview plains.

bosses.name: Fallingstar Beast
bosses.lore: A creature that fell from the stars and burrowed into the earth of Scaduview, attacking with gravitational force and magnetic precision.

bosses.name: Scadutree Avatar
bosses.lore: The avatar of the Scadutree itself — a divine being of immense power that embodies the blessing of the Shadow Realm's sacred tree.

bosses.name: Tree Sentinel
bosses.lore: A golden tree sentinel of the Shadow Realm, mounted on an armored horse and wielding a halberd in service to the Scadutree.

bosses.name: Divine Beast Dancing Lion
bosses.lore: A second divine beast dancing lion encountered in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh, more powerful than its counterpart in the Gravesite Plain.

bosses.name: Romina, Saint of the Bud
bosses.lore: A saint transformed into a spider-centaur form by the Scarlet Rot, her golden hair now tangled with blooming rot blossoms of terrible beauty.

bosses.name: Leda and Allies
bosses.lore: Needle Knight Leda and a coalition of Miquella's followers who have lost themselves to his charm, fighting against the player at the threshold of the final sanctum.

bosses.name: Promised Consort Radahn
bosses.lore: Radahn reborn as Miquella's consort, his body and soul devoted entirely to his god, wielding cross-slash attacks of light and gravity in the most brutal final boss of the DLC.

bosses.name: Margit, the Fell Omen
bosses.lore: A powerful demigod guarding the path to Stormveil Castle. He wields a magical hammer and swift blade techniques.

bosses.name: Grafted Scion
bosses.lore: A nightmarish creature made of grafted limbs and weapons, serving as the opening gauntlet of Elden Ring before the player is sent back to the tutorial.

bosses.name: Soldier of Godrick
bosses.lore: A basic soldier serving as the tutorial's final test — Godrick's standard-bearer who guards the exit to the Stranded Graveyard.

bosses.name: Beastman of Farum Azula
bosses.lore: A beastman warrior from Farum Azula imprisoned in a Limgrave cave, powerful and feral, wielding a massive curved sword with bestial fury.

bosses.name: Bell Bearing Hunter
bosses.lore: A spectral hunter that appears at night to protect bell bearings from being sold, wielding a colossal blade with terrifying aggression.

bosses.name: Black Knife Assassin
bosses.lore: A silent assassin from the Black Knife conspiracy, wielding a blade imbued with the Rune of Death — a knife that can steal life itself.

bosses.name: Bloodhound Knight Darriwil
bosses.lore: A disgraced bloodhound knight imprisoned in the Forlorn Hound Evergaol, whose bestial speed and bloodhound step make him a terrifying early challenge.

bosses.name: Crucible Knight
bosses.lore: An ancient knight of the Crucible who commands primordial incantations — tail swipes, wings, and horns — armored in nearly impenetrable Erdtree plate.

bosses.name: Deathbird
bosses.lore: A massive spectral bird that appears only at night, a servant of death performing rites to collect the souls of the fallen.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Chief
bosses.lore: The chief of a demi-human clan dwelling in Limgrave's coastal caves, a cunning fighter who commands his kin while wielding a stone club.

bosses.name: Erdtree Burial Watchdog
bosses.lore: A stone lion statue animated by the Erdtree's power to guard burial grounds, capable of breathing flame and swiping with enormous stone paws.

bosses.name: Flying Dragon Agheel
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon that has made its lair in the Dragon-Burnt Ruins, a fearsome early test that can torch an unprepared player from the air.

bosses.name: Godrick the Grafted
bosses.lore: A demigod who grafts the limbs of fallen warriors onto his own body, seeking power he was never born with.

bosses.name: Grave Warden Duelist
bosses.lore: A massive warrior charged with guarding the catacombs who fights with heavy chains swung as devastating flails.

bosses.name: Guardian Golem
bosses.lore: A colossal stone golem placed to guard a cave passage in Limgrave, slow but capable of crushing attacks that devastate the unwary.

bosses.name: Mad Pumpkin Head
bosses.lore: A warrior whose head is encased in a spiked iron pumpkin cage — a form of punishment — who attacks with wild, headbutting aggression.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A ghostly mounted knight that patrols only at night, wielding a halberd on horseback. It vanishes at dawn.

bosses.name: Patches
bosses.lore: A cunning rogue who ambushes treasure-seekers and pushes them into pits, only to feign remorse afterwards. A recurring trickster across FromSoftware games.

bosses.name: Stonedigger Troll
bosses.lore: A massive troll that digs through stone with a stone club, imprisoned in the Limgrave Tunnels — a simple but brutally powerful beast.

bosses.name: Tibia Mariner
bosses.lore: A skeletal boatman of death who rows through the land raising the undead, a summoner boss that must be killed to end the endless reinforcements.

bosses.name: Tree Sentinel
bosses.lore: A golden knight on horseback patrolling outside the Church of Elleh — often the first wall new players hit, rewarding patience and skill.

bosses.name: Ulcerated Tree Spirit
bosses.lore: A tree spirit driven mad by rot and corruption, twisted into a writhing serpentine mass that was once a guardian of grace.

bosses.name: Ancient Hero of Zamor
bosses.lore: A frost giant from the ancient civilization of Zamor, sealed away in an evergaol. His frostbite incantations were legendary among the giants.

bosses.name: Cemetery Shade
bosses.lore: A shadowy entity that guards the cemeteries, teleporting rapidly and attacking with slashing spectral appendages.

bosses.name: Deathbird
bosses.lore: A massive spectral death rite bird patrolling the Weeping Peninsula at night, conducting its dark rituals over the coastal graves.

bosses.name: Erdtree Avatar
bosses.lore: A massive avatar of the Erdtree protecting a Minor Erdtree, wielding a blossoming staff of immense power.

bosses.name: Erdtree Burial Watchdog
bosses.lore: A dual-headed stone watchdog guarding the Impaler's Catacombs, capable of breathing fire and launching swiping combinations.

bosses.name: Leonine Misbegotten
bosses.lore: A lion-headed misbegotten warrior at the tip of the Weeping Peninsula, wielding a massive greatsword with bestial fury from the cliffs of Castle Morne.

bosses.name: Miranda the Blighted Bloom
bosses.lore: A giant poisonous flower that fires a barrage of toxic spores and sap, its beauty masking a deadly intelligence.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A ghostly mounted knight patrolling the roads of the Weeping Peninsula after dark, a more aggressive variant of its Limgrave counterpart.

bosses.name: Runebear
bosses.lore: An enormous bear of immense aggression imprisoned in an evergaol on the Weeping Peninsula — perhaps the most dangerous encounter relative to its location.

bosses.name: Scaly Misbegotten
bosses.lore: A scaly, reptilian misbegotten wielding a large weapon, found near Castle Morne among the other misbegotten of the region.

bosses.name: Adan, Thief of Fire
bosses.lore: A thief imprisoned in an evergaol for stealing flame incantations. He fights with pyromancies and a flail, a criminal turned dangerous sorcerer.

bosses.name: Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader
bosses.lore: The ringleader of the Black Knife Assassins, imprisoned in an evergaol in Liurnia — incredibly aggressive with teleporting phantom strikes.

bosses.name: Bell Bearing Hunter
bosses.lore: A night-spawning spectral hunter prowling Liurnia in search of bell bearings, wielding a colossal greatsword with devastating reach.

bosses.name: Black Knife Assassin
bosses.lore: A stealthy Black Knife Assassin guarding an ancient site in Liurnia, their blade carrying a fragment of the Rune of Death.

bosses.name: Bloodhound Knight
bosses.lore: A bloodhound knight lurking in a Liurnia dungeon, using the bestial bloodhound step to close distance with terrifying speed.

bosses.name: Bols, Carian Knight
bosses.lore: A giant Carian knight wielding a colossal club, a loyal but towering guardian of the Carian study hall in Liurnia.

bosses.name: Cemetery Shade
bosses.lore: A shadowy entity guarding a Liurnia cemetery, its translucent form flickering as it attacks with spectral claws.

bosses.name: Cleanrot Knight
bosses.lore: An elite knight of Malenia's army found in Liurnia, wielding sword and spear with disciplined and aggressive precision.

bosses.name: Crystalian
bosses.lore: A crystalline being formed from the academy's sorcery research, near-immune to slashing weapons but shattering to blunt force.

bosses.name: Crystalian Spear and Crystalian Staff
bosses.lore: A duo of Crystalians wielding a spear and magic staff — combining physical and sorcery attacks in a challenging tag-team.

bosses.name: Death Rite Bird
bosses.lore: A death rite bird conducting its funerary rituals in Liurnia's cold waters, wielding a staff of ghostly blue flame.

bosses.name: Deathbird
bosses.lore: A spectral death rite bird that appears at night in Liurnia, its enormous wingspan casting shadows over the moonlit lake.

bosses.name: Erdtree Avatar
bosses.lore: A Minor Erdtree's guardian avatar in the northeast of Liurnia, protecting its tree from all who would harm the golden order.

bosses.name: Erdtree Avatar
bosses.lore: A Minor Erdtree's guardian avatar in the southwest of Liurnia, its staff crackling with golden light.

bosses.name: Erdtree Burial Watchdog
bosses.lore: A stone lion animated by Erdtree power to guard the catacombs of Liurnia, attacking with flame breath and crushing stone blows.

bosses.name: Glintstone Dragon Adula
bosses.lore: Ranni's ancient dragon companion, Adula is a glintstone dragon who breathes sorcerous blue crystal and guards the path to the Three Sisters.

bosses.name: Glintstone Dragon Smarag
bosses.lore: A glintstone dragon sleeping near the Academy's main gate, one of the ancient dragons that has attuned to the sorcery of Raya Lucaria.

bosses.name: Magma Wyrm Makar
bosses.lore: A magma wyrm blocking the underground river passage to Altus Plateau, wielding a curved greatsword while spewing lava.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A ghostly mounted knight that patrols the northern reaches of Liurnia at night, wielding a great bident with sweeping reaches.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A spectral cavalry knight patrolling the southern roads of Liurnia after dark, its mount and rider both suffused with ghostly light.

bosses.name: Omenkiller
bosses.lore: A masked executioner who hunts omen-born across the Lands Between, wielding blood-soaked cleavers with practiced brutality.

bosses.name: Onyx Lord
bosses.lore: A celestial lord who commands gravitational magic, falling from the stars long ago and now dwelling in a divine tower in Liurnia.

bosses.name: Red Wolf of Radagon
bosses.lore: A magical construct serving as guardian of the academy, wielding sorcery and devastating physical attacks.

bosses.name: Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon
bosses.lore: Former head of the academy and wielder of the Full Moon sorcery, now a broken figure clutching her amber egg.

bosses.name: Royal Knight Loretta
bosses.lore: The phantom guardian of the Carian Manor, a loyal knight who wields a halberd and glintstone sorcery to guard the path to the Three Sisters.

bosses.name: Royal Revenant
bosses.lore: A spider-limbed revenant of Carian royalty, scuttling with unsettling speed and spitting poison while clawing with multiple arms.

bosses.name: Spirit-Caller Snail
bosses.lore: An invisible snail that summons powerful spirit warriors to fight on its behalf — the real boss is finding and killing the hidden mollusc.

bosses.name: Tibia Mariner
bosses.lore: A skeletal death-rite boatman who rows through the shores of Liurnia raising undead soldiers in endless waves.

bosses.name: Cemetery Shade
bosses.lore: A shadowy entity guarding Caelid's cursed catacombs, flickering between visibility as it lashes out with spectral tendrils.

bosses.name: Commander O'Neil
bosses.lore: A former military commander whose army has been consumed by Scarlet Rot, now wielding a halberd and summoning his decaying soldiers in waves.

bosses.name: Crucible Knight and Misbegotten Warrior
bosses.lore: An ancient Crucible Knight paired with a feral Misbegotten Warrior — one of the game's most challenging optional duo encounters.

bosses.name: Death Rite Bird
bosses.lore: A death rite bird performing its dark ceremonies in Caelid's rot-stricken wastes, its ghostly flame corrupted by the Scarlet Rot.

bosses.name: Decaying Ekzykes
bosses.lore: A dragon so ravaged by Scarlet Rot that it has become a rotting hulk, breathing great gouts of infectious scarlet rot across Caelid.

bosses.name: Erdtree Burial Watchdog Duo
bosses.lore: Two stone burial watchdogs working in tandem, coordinating their fire breath and paw swipes in a challenging double encounter.

bosses.name: Fallingstar Beast
bosses.lore: A creature of gravitational power that fell from the stars and burrowed into the Caelid cliffs, attacking with magnetic force and crystal horns.

bosses.name: Frenzied Duelist
bosses.lore: A gladiator driven to madness by the Frenzied Flame, wielding heavy weapons with reckless and unpredictable aggression in Caelid's arena.

bosses.name: Mad Pumpkin Heads
bosses.lore: A pair of pumpkin-helmed warriors working together in a narrow space, their iron headbutts and wide swings covering each other's flanks.

bosses.name: Magma Wyrm
bosses.lore: A magma wyrm that dwells in the volcanic caves of Caelid, slithering through pools of lava and striking with its curved draconic blade.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A spectral mounted knight that rides the haunted roads of Caelid at night, its armor stained with the red dust of the rot-blasted wastes.

bosses.name: Nox Swordstress and Nox Monk
bosses.lore: Two Nox warriors who guard the Sellia hideaway — a graceful swordstress and a staff-wielding monk who fight in complementary styles.

bosses.name: Putrid Avatar
bosses.lore: A Minor Erdtree avatar corrupted by Scarlet Rot, its formerly golden frame now dripping with rot as it protects a diseased tree.

bosses.name: Starscourge Radahn
bosses.lore: One of the greatest demigods, a warrior who held the stars in place through gravitational sorcery.

bosses.name: Battlemage Hugues
bosses.lore: A powerful battlemage sealed in an evergaol in the Dragonbarrow, combining fearsome sorcery with close-range combat ability.

bosses.name: Beastman of Farum Azula Duo
bosses.lore: Two beastmen from Farum Azula imprisoned together in the Dragonbarrow, their coordinated fury making them far deadlier than either alone.

bosses.name: Bell Bearing Hunter
bosses.lore: A spectral night-hunter that appears at the Bestial Sanctum in Dragonbarrow, wielding a black blade of enormous size with savage speed.

bosses.name: Black Blade Kindred
bosses.lore: A gargoyle-like servant of Maliketh wielding a black blade imbued with destined death, guarding the entrance to the Bestial Sanctum.

bosses.name: Cleanrot Knight Duo
bosses.lore: Two Cleanrot Knights of Malenia's army fighting in concert, their coordinated spear and sword attacks leaving little room to breathe.

bosses.name: Flying Dragon Greyll
bosses.lore: A massive ancient dragon that dominates the bridge of the Dragonbarrow, breathing fire and crushing everything beneath its colossal bulk.

bosses.name: Godskin Apostle
bosses.lore: A Godskin Apostle who has claimed part of the Dragonbarrow, able to extend its body into a whip-like form and roll across the arena.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A spectral cavalry knight that rides the cursed roads of Dragonbarrow at night, armored more heavily than its counterparts in other regions.

bosses.name: Putrid Avatar
bosses.lore: A rot-corrupted Erdtree avatar lurking in the Dragonbarrow, its formerly protective role twisted by the Scarlet Rot that dominates Caelid.

bosses.name: Putrid Crystalian Trio
bosses.lore: Three Crystalians corrupted by Scarlet Rot, their crystalline forms now infused with rot — fought simultaneously in a claustrophobic dungeon.

bosses.name: Putrid Tree Spirit
bosses.lore: A tree spirit twisted by Scarlet Rot, writhing with infected energy in addition to its normal crushing and biting attacks.

bosses.name: Ancient Dragon Lansseax
bosses.lore: A massive ancient dragon who was a close companion of Maliketh, marked with the black blade — she is found wounded and slowly dying on the plateau.

bosses.name: Ancient Hero of Zamor
bosses.lore: An ancient frost giant hero from Zamor imprisoned on the plateau, his frost incantations still formidable after untold centuries of imprisonment.

bosses.name: Black Knife Assassin
bosses.lore: A Black Knife Assassin lurking in Sage's Cave on the Altus Plateau, fighting alongside a mimic to confuse and overwhelm intruders.

bosses.name: Black Knife Assassin
bosses.lore: A Black Knife Assassin guarding the Sainted Hero's Grave, attacking swiftly from shadows with their blade imbued with the Rune of Death.

bosses.name: Crystalian Spear and Crystalian Ringblade
bosses.lore: A duo of Crystalians in the Altus Plateau dungeon, one thrusting with a spear and one spinning a razor ringblade with deadly precision.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Queen Gilika
bosses.lore: A demi-human queen dwelling in the Altus Plateau's ravines, leading her tribe with crude but potent magical capabilities.

bosses.name: Elemer of the Briar
bosses.lore: A lord imprisoned in the Shaded Castle who commands his sword and shield to fight from across the room through telekinesis and magnetic force.

bosses.name: Erdtree Burial Watchdog
bosses.lore: A stone burial watchdog guarding a catacomb on the Altus Plateau, one of the more powerful variants with enhanced fire breath.

bosses.name: Fallingstar Beast
bosses.lore: A creature of gravitational power that fell from the stars onto the Altus Plateau, its crystal horns and magnetic field making it a deadly obstacle.

bosses.name: Godefroy the Grafted
bosses.lore: A copy of Godrick the Grafted imprisoned in an evergaol — equally grotesque with grafted limbs, equally desperate for strength.

bosses.name: Godskin Apostle
bosses.lore: A Godskin Apostle who has infiltrated a holy tower on the Altus Plateau, its elastic body posing an unusual challenge in the confined tower top.

bosses.name: Necromancer Garris
bosses.lore: A necromancer sealed in an evergaol who raises undead minions as shields while striking from range with powerful dark sorceries.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A ghostly mounted knight riding the roads of the Altus Plateau at night, wielding a barbed staff of lightning with tremendous reach.

bosses.name: Omenkiller and Miranda the Blighted Bloom
bosses.lore: A masked omen-executioner paired with a giant poisonous flower — a brutal duo with both melee and area-of-effect poison pressure.

bosses.name: Perfumer Tricia and Misbegotten Warrior
bosses.lore: A perfumer mage paired with a feral misbegotten warrior, combining ranged flask-throwing with the misbegotten's savage melee attacks.

bosses.name: Sanguine Noble
bosses.lore: A blood-magic noble sealed in an evergaol who attacks with blood whips and blood sorceries of considerable power.

bosses.name: Stonedigger Troll
bosses.lore: A mining troll imprisoned in the Altus Plateau tunnels, swinging a stone club with enough force to crack the cavern walls.

bosses.name: Tibia Mariner
bosses.lore: A death-rite skeletal mariner conducting its rituals on the Altus Plateau, raising skeletons and even a giant skeleton to overwhelm attackers.

bosses.name: Tree Sentinel Duo
bosses.lore: Two golden tree sentinels guarding the main gate to Leyndell, mounted and coordinated — the definitive checkpoint before the Royal Capital.

bosses.name: Wormface
bosses.lore: A horrifying humanoid whose face and torso are composed of writhing worms that breathe death blight, lurking in the blighted forests of the plateau.

bosses.name: Abductor Virgins Duo
bosses.lore: Two Abductor Virgins — iron maiden-like creatures that slam shut around victims — working together in the Volcano Manor's depths.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Queen Maggie
bosses.lore: A demi-human queen who has claimed a portion of Mt. Gelmir, wielding sorcery that belies her chaotic appearance.

bosses.name: Demi-Human Queen Margot
bosses.lore: Another demi-human queen of Mt. Gelmir, a counterpart to Maggie, commanding the demi-human tribes of the volcanic mountain.

bosses.name: Full-Grown Fallingstar Beast
bosses.lore: A mature Fallingstar Beast at the peak of Mt. Gelmir — larger and more dangerous than the ones found below, its gravitational powers fully developed.

bosses.name: Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy
bosses.lore: A demigod who fed himself to a serpent god, merging with it to gain immortality at the cost of his humanity.

bosses.name: Godskin Noble
bosses.lore: A Godskin Noble who has taken up residence in the Volcano Manor, its rotund body deceptively agile as it inflates and rolls without warning.

bosses.name: Kindred of Rot Duo
bosses.lore: Two Kindred of Rot — insectoid creatures of the Scarlet Rot — fighting together in the tunnels beneath Mt. Gelmir.

bosses.name: Magma Wyrm
bosses.lore: A magma wyrm that guards the approaches to Fort Laiedd, wading through volcanic rock as if it were water.

bosses.name: Red Wolf of the Champion
bosses.lore: A red wolf similar to Radagon's Red Wolf, this variant is a champion-tier enemy imprisoned in an evergaol in the volcanic mountain.

bosses.name: Ulcerated Tree Spirit
bosses.lore: A tree spirit consumed by rot and corruption lurking in the Volcano Manor, its thrashing body filling the arena with terrible range.

bosses.name: Bell Bearing Hunter
bosses.lore: A spectral night hunter prowling the roads around Leyndell, appearing at night to attack anyone near the bell-bearing merchant's location.

bosses.name: Crucible Knight and Crucible Knight Ordovis
bosses.lore: Two ancient Crucible Knights fighting together — Ordovis bears wings and a colossal sword, while his partner attacks with a shield and spear.

bosses.name: Deathbird
bosses.lore: A massive death rite bird that haunts the outskirts of Leyndell at night, one of the most powerful variants of the creature.

bosses.name: Draconic Tree Sentinel
bosses.lore: A Tree Sentinel enhanced by draconic power, wielding a lightning-imbued halberd and capable of calling down lightning strikes.

bosses.name: Fell Twins
bosses.lore: Twin omen warriors who guard the underground approach to Leyndell, wielding massive axes with synchronized aggression.

bosses.name: Grave Warden Duelist
bosses.lore: A massive chain-wielding duelist guarding the catacombs beneath the Capital Outskirts.

bosses.name: Onyx Lord
bosses.lore: A celestial lord of gravitational magic found in the catacombs near Leyndell, commanding rock and starfall with equal ease.

bosses.name: Esgar, Priest of Blood
bosses.lore: A blood-magic priest lurking in the sewers beneath Leyndell, a practitioner of Mohg's bloodflame who serves as a dungeon boss beneath the capital.

bosses.name: Godfrey, First Elden Lord
bosses.lore: The golden phantom of the First Elden Lord — a projection of Godfrey's warrior spirit that tests the Tarnished before the true king.

bosses.name: Mohg, the Omen
bosses.lore: An Omen variant of Mohg discovered beneath the sewers of Leyndell — an earlier, perhaps lesser form of the Lord of Blood.

bosses.name: Morgott, the Omen King
bosses.lore: The true identity of Margit — an Omen cursed with thorns who locked himself away to protect the Erdtree.

bosses.name: Borealis the Freezing Fog
bosses.lore: A dragon whose breath has become pure frozen fog, blanketing the Mountaintops in blizzard conditions and freezing all who wander near.

bosses.name: Commander Niall
bosses.lore: A veteran military commander who summons two spectral knights before fighting himself, wielding a lightning pike with exceptional skill.

bosses.name: Death Rite Bird
bosses.lore: A death rite bird performing its ceremonies in the frozen Mountaintops, its black flame breath supercharged by the sacred environment.

bosses.name: Erdtree Avatar
bosses.lore: A Minor Erdtree avatar on the frozen Mountaintops, guarding one of the last living trees near the forge of the giants.

bosses.name: Fire Giant
bosses.lore: The last of a race of giants, kept alive by the Greater Will to guard the Forge of the Giants.

bosses.name: Godskin Apostle and Godskin Noble
bosses.lore: A Spirit-Caller Snail summons both a Godskin Apostle and Godskin Noble simultaneously — an omen of the duo encounter yet to come in Farum Azula.

bosses.name: Ancient Hero of Zamor
bosses.lore: A powerful Zamor hero imprisoned in the icy wastes of the Mountaintops, his frost magic particularly potent in the frozen environment.

bosses.name: Ulcerated Tree Spirit
bosses.lore: A tree spirit thrashing in the frozen wastes of the Mountaintops, its body steaming where rot meets the bitter cold.

bosses.name: Vyke, Knight of the Roundtable
bosses.lore: A former Roundtable Hold champion who was driven mad by the Frenzied Flame, now an invader wielding his signature lightning spear.

bosses.name: Beast Clergyman / Maliketh, the Black Blade
bosses.lore: Destined death incarnate, the Black Blade Kindred and shadow of Queen Marika.

bosses.name: Dragonlord Placidusax
bosses.lore: A two-headed ancient dragon who was once the Elden Lord before Marika's time, frozen in prayer at the edge of the time-lost city of Farum Azula.

bosses.name: Godskin Duo
bosses.lore: Two members of the Godskin Apostles working in tandem — Noble and Apostle — servants of the Black Blade Kindred.

bosses.name: Black Blade Kindred
bosses.lore: A gargoyle servant of Maliketh that guards the gate to the Forbidden Lands and Mountaintops, wielding a black blade of death.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry
bosses.lore: A spectral cavalry knight riding the Forbidden Lands at night — unusual in appearing even in this desolate and transitional zone.

bosses.name: Stray Mimic Tear
bosses.lore: A stray Mimic Tear that has taken a vaguely humanoid form, wandering the Forbidden Lands in an incomplete imitation of life.

bosses.name: Astel, Stars of Darkness
bosses.lore: A second Astel found in the Consecrated Snowfield, possibly the same creature or a kin — this variant commands darkness instead of the void.

bosses.name: Death Rite Bird
bosses.lore: A death rite bird performing its mournful ceremonies in the frozen Consecrated Snowfield, its black flame intensified by the sacred cold.

bosses.name: Great Wyrm Theodorix
bosses.lore: An enormous magma wyrm who has grown to legendary size in the Consecrated Snowfield, leaving trails of lava across the frozen tundra.

bosses.name: Misbegotten Crusader
bosses.lore: A misbegotten warrior wielding a golden sword, imprisoned in the Cave of the Forlorn — one of the more unusual misbegotten encounters.

bosses.name: Night's Cavalry Duo
bosses.lore: Two spectral cavalry knights riding together through the Consecrated Snowfield at night — one of the most punishing night encounters in the game.

bosses.name: Putrid Avatar
bosses.lore: An avatar of a Minor Erdtree in the snowfield, corrupted by Scarlet Rot that has spread even into this frozen sacred place.

bosses.name: Putrid Grave Warden Duelist
bosses.lore: A chain-wielding grave warden duelist in the snowfield's catacombs, their chains infused with Scarlet Rot.

bosses.name: Loretta, Knight of the Haligtree
bosses.lore: An upgraded version of Royal Knight Loretta who now serves as guardian of the Haligtree entrance, wielding both halberd and glintblade sorceries.

bosses.name: Malenia, Blade of Miquella
bosses.lore: Universally regarded as the hardest boss in Elden Ring. She has never known defeat, and every hit she lands heals her.

bosses.name: Ancestor Spirit
bosses.lore: The spirit of a great ancestral stag that once presided over the underground Siofra River, awakened by lighting the eight ritual fires.

bosses.name: Dragonkin Soldier
bosses.lore: A soldier of the dragonkin who guards the Siofra River basin, wielding a massive lightning weapon suited to the underground lake.

bosses.name: Mohg, Lord of Blood
bosses.lore: An Omen demigod who consorts with the Formless Mother, dreaming of a dynasty built on rivers of blood.

bosses.name: Dragonkin Soldier of Nokstella
bosses.lore: A dragonkin soldier that guards the depths of Nokstella in the Ainsel River, a larger and more formidable variant of its Siofra counterpart.

bosses.name: Mimic Tear
bosses.lore: A creature of silver tear that has perfectly replicated the player's appearance, equipment, and attack patterns — the ultimate mirror match.

bosses.name: Regal Ancestor Spirit
bosses.lore: A more powerful version of the Ancestor Spirit found in Nokron's eternal city, its ancient power magnified by the sacred underground ruins.

bosses.name: Valiant Gargoyle and Valiant Gargoyle Twinblade
bosses.lore: Two stone gargoyles guarding the coffin passage to Deeproot Depths — one wields a greataxe and twin blades, requiring endurance to overcome.

bosses.name: Crucible Knight Siluria
bosses.lore: An ancient Crucible Knight who wields the primordial Siluria's Tree spear, one of the most powerful relics of the Crucible age.

bosses.name: Fia's Champions
bosses.lore: A succession of phantom warriors summoned by Fia, Deathbed Companion — each falls only for the next to arrive, testing the player's resources.

bosses.name: Lichdragon Fortissax
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon and close companion of Godwyn the Golden who refused to abandon his friend's corpse to the Death, fighting within a dream.

bosses.name: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
bosses.lore: A creature that fell from the stars and obliterated an entire civilisation. It can clone itself and explode stars.

bosses.name: Dragonkin Soldier
bosses.lore: A dragonkin soldier wading through the Lake of Rot, its powerful frame seemingly immune to the Scarlet Rot that fills the cavern.

bosses.name: Esgar, Priest of Blood
bosses.lore: The blood priest Esgar persists even in the ashen capital, a phantom of the city's corrupted past lurking in the underground.

bosses.name: Godfrey, First Elden Lord / Hoarah Loux
bosses.lore: The true form of Godfrey, First Elden Lord — a savage chieftain before he was graced by the Erdtree.

bosses.name: Mohg, the Omen
bosses.lore: The Omen form of Mohg that lurks beneath the ashen capital, his bloodflame mixing with the ash of the burned Erdtree above.

bosses.name: Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing
bosses.lore: The Roundtable's spymaster who has forsaken the Tarnished cause, wielding copied sorceries and incantations stolen from every demigod he studied.

bosses.name: Radagon of the Golden Order / Elden Beast
bosses.lore: The vessel of the Elden Ring itself — first Radagon, the secret second self of Marika, then the Elden Beast: an outer god that descended from the Erdtree to face the Tarnished.

bosses.name: Iudex Gundyr
bosses.lore: A Champion who failed his duty and was impaled with a coiled sword, waiting for the Ashen One to rekindle the flame.

bosses.name: Vordt of the Boreal Valley
bosses.lore: A former knight of the Pontiff turned beast, corrupted by the Sulyvahn's eyes and now serving as a mindless guardian.

bosses.name: Curse-rotted Greatwood
bosses.lore: A colossal tree that absorbed the curses of the undead for generations until the accumulated rot animated it as a grotesque giant.

bosses.name: Crystal Sage
bosses.lore: A master sorcerer of the Grand Archives who can teleport and split into multiple illusions to confuse attackers.

bosses.name: Deacons of the Deep
bosses.lore: A congregation of clerics who collectively wield a great soul, protecting Aldrich's coffin in the cathedral.

bosses.name: Abyss Watchers
bosses.lore: An order of warriors who hunted the abyss, but one was infected — now they fight each other endlessly.

bosses.name: High Lord Wolnir
bosses.lore: Once a conquering king who feared death above all, he sought the abyss seeking immortality and was consumed by it.

bosses.name: Old Demon King
bosses.lore: The last of the chaos demons, a decrepit king smouldering with dying embers, a relic of the Age of Fire.

bosses.name: Pontiff Sulyvahn
bosses.lore: A sorcerer-king who enslaved Aldrich and Yhorm, and whose magical eyes grant visions of the Profaned Capital.

bosses.name: Yhorm the Giant
bosses.lore: A giant king who took the Profaned Flame to end the suffering of his people. His shield is the tomb of their tragedies.

bosses.name: Aldrich, Devourer of Gods
bosses.lore: A cleric who dreamed of feasting on men and gods alike, eventually consuming the God of the Sun, Gwyndolin.

bosses.name: Dancer of the Boreal Valley
bosses.lore: A former human knight cursed by Sulyvahn and transformed into a graceful but deadly dancer of ash and frost.

bosses.name: Dragonslayer Armour
bosses.lore: The animated armour of a legendary dragonslayer, now empty of its wearer but still driven to protect the path ahead.

bosses.name: Oceiros, the Consumed King
bosses.lore: The king of Lothric who became obsessed with dragons, eventually transforming into a dragon himself.

bosses.name: Champion Gundyr
bosses.lore: Iudex Gundyr in a dark timeline where the fire was never linked — a relentless fighter driven by pure instinct.

bosses.name: The Nameless King
bosses.lore: A deity who forsook his divine status to live among dragons, wielding lightning and riding a wyvern in battle.

bosses.name: Ancient Wyvern
bosses.lore: A massive ancient wyvern guarding Archdragon Peak, defeated not through combat but through a plunging attack from above.

bosses.name: Twin Princes
bosses.lore: Lorian and Lothric — the elder brothers the younger, who refuses to link the flame. Two souls fighting as one body.

bosses.name: Soul of Cinder
bosses.lore: The amalgamation of every Lord of Cinder who ever linked the First Flame — the final guardian of the Age of Fire.

bosses.name: Sister Friede
bosses.lore: A former Sable Church member who abandoned everything to protect a painted world, fighting alongside Father Ariandel.

bosses.name: Champion's Gravetender and Gravetender Greatwolf
bosses.lore: A lone champion and his faithful greatwolf who guard the painted world's secrets together in the frozen wastes.

bosses.name: Demon Prince
bosses.lore: The last of the demons, two demon lords merged into one being, fighting in the ashes at the edge of the world.

bosses.name: Halflight, Spear of the Church
bosses.lore: A loyal Spear of the Church who defends the inner sanctum of the Ringed City from invaders, aided by the gods.

bosses.name: Darkeater Midir
bosses.lore: A dragon raised by the gods to consume the darkness of the Abyss — after aeons, the Abyss consumed him instead.

bosses.name: Slave Knight Gael
bosses.lore: A slave knight sent to find the Dark Soul of Humanity for his Lady. He consumed every last dark soul he could find.

bosses.name: Asylum Demon
bosses.lore: The first boss of the game — a massive demon that crashes through the floor before the player has any weapons.

bosses.name: Taurus Demon
bosses.lore: A bull-headed demon standing atop a tower gate, a relic of chaos from the Witch of Izalith's failed attempt to recreate the First Flame.

bosses.name: Bell Gargoyles
bosses.lore: Twin gargoyles that guard the first bell of awakening atop the Undead Parish church, the first true co-op boss of the series.

bosses.name: Moonlight Butterfly
bosses.lore: A magical creature born from sorcery experiments at Seath's archives, fluttering serenely while firing lethal crystal bolts.

bosses.name: Capra Demon
bosses.lore: A goat-headed demon notorious for its cramped arena and two attack dogs that ambush the player the moment the fog gate opens.

bosses.name: Gaping Dragon
bosses.lore: A dragon so overcome by its hunger that its torso split open into an enormous maw, transforming it into a grotesque feeding machine.

bosses.name: Chaos Witch Quelaag
bosses.lore: A daughter of the Witch of Izalith, half-woman half-spider, who fled to Blighttown to care for her ill sister amid the toxic swamps.

bosses.name: Ceaseless Discharge
bosses.lore: A demon born from the chaos flame that perpetually weeps magma from its wounds, driven to madness by unending pain.

bosses.name: Demon Firesage
bosses.lore: One of the original demons from the Witch of Izalith's failed ritual, an elder sage whose power has faded with the dying flames.

bosses.name: Centipede Demon
bosses.lore: A demon formed from countless centipedes fused together into a writhing mass that can detach limbs and regrow them instantly.

bosses.name: Bed of Chaos
bosses.lore: The corrupted remains of the Witch of Izalith herself, fused with the chaos flame she tried to recreate, now an endlessly spawning source of demons.

bosses.name: Iron Golem
bosses.lore: A massive mechanical golem forged by the ancient gods as a guardian for the road to Anor Londo, wielding a greataxe with seismic force.

bosses.name: Dragon Slayer Ornstein and Executioner Smough
bosses.lore: Two of Gwyn's most loyal knights — the swift spear-wielder Ornstein and the brutal hammer-wielding cannibal Smough — the definitive Souls duo boss.

bosses.name: Sif, the Great Grey Wolf
bosses.lore: The loyal companion of Knight Artorias, guarding his grave with unwavering devotion. She fights despite her wounds to protect her master's legacy.

bosses.name: Stray Demon
bosses.lore: A demon that escaped its shackles in the asylum, wandering the upper floor with chaotic explosive attacks.

bosses.name: Pinwheel
bosses.lore: A necromancer who stole the power of the Gravelord Nito to resurrect his dead family, splitting himself into three masked personas.

bosses.name: Crossbreed Priscilla
bosses.lore: A half-dragon, half-god being exiled to the painted world for being an unwanted crossbreed. She asks only to be left in peace.

bosses.name: Gravelord Nito
bosses.lore: The first of the dead, a skeletal deity draped in countless corpses who commands death and disease as one of the four Lords of Cinder.

bosses.name: Seath the Scaleless
bosses.lore: A scaleless dragon who betrayed his own kind out of jealousy, becoming the Duke of Anor Londo and pursuing immortality through crystal sorcery.

bosses.name: Four Kings
bosses.lore: The ghost kings of New Londo, granted a piece of the Dark Soul by Gwyn and corrupted by the Abyss that Artorias had to contain.

bosses.name: Sanctuary Guardian
bosses.lore: A chimeric creature — part lion, part eagle, part scorpion — that guards the entrance to Oolacile in the Artorias of the Abyss DLC.

bosses.name: Knight Artorias
bosses.lore: A legendary knight of Gwyn who dove into the Abyss to save Oolacile, and was consumed by it — his left arm broken, his will corrupted.

bosses.name: Manus, Father of the Abyss
bosses.lore: An ancient primordial human whose buried humanity went feral, tearing a rift in time and space and becoming the Dark Soul incarnate.

bosses.name: Black Dragon Kalameet
bosses.lore: One of the few true ancient dragons to survive the war with the gods, bearing a calamity mark and fought only with the help of Knight Hawkeye Gough.

bosses.name: Gwyn, Lord of Cinder
bosses.lore: The greatest of the lords who linked the First Flame, now a hollow shell of cinder with faded power but relentless aggression, who parries your attacks.

bosses.name: The Last Giant
bosses.lore: A colossal giant imprisoned after a great war, who tears off his own arm to fight after you destroy his shackles.

bosses.name: The Pursuer
bosses.lore: A knight who relentlessly hunts the Bearer of the Curse across the entire game, appearing multiple times before a final confrontation.

bosses.name: Dragonrider
bosses.lore: A loyal knight of King Vendrick who once rode dragons into battle, now standing sentinel in the flooded ruins of Heide.

bosses.name: Old Dragonslayer
bosses.lore: A revenant version of Ornstein from Dark Souls 1, somehow drawn across worlds, wielding the same spear but with dark energy.

bosses.name: Flexile Sentry
bosses.lore: A creature of two bodies fused at the waist — one human, one serpentine — serving as jailor of No-man's Wharf.

bosses.name: Ruin Sentinels
bosses.lore: Three hollow knights that drop from the ceiling to overwhelm the player, relics of a kingdom that valued order above all.

bosses.name: Belfry Gargoyles
bosses.lore: A swarm of animated stone gargoyles that multiply until destroyed, protecting the bells of Drangleic in the belfry tower.

bosses.name: The Lost Sinner
bosses.lore: A once-great sorcerer who tried to rekindle the First Flame in an act deemed sinful, now imprisoned and blind but still formidable.

bosses.name: Skeleton Lords
bosses.lore: Three skeletal lords of Drangleic who each spawn waves of smaller skeletons upon death, creating a chaotic multi-phase encounter.

bosses.name: Covetous Demon
bosses.lore: A man driven mad by his desperate love for the Queen, who ate everything around him until his body transformed into a bloated demon.

bosses.name: Mytha, the Baneful Queen
bosses.lore: A queen who poisoned herself trying to become beautiful for her king, now a snake-bodied horror presiding over a windmill of poison.

bosses.name: Smelter Demon
bosses.lore: A mechanical iron demon that absorbs heat until its entire body becomes a blazing furnace, a terrifying early obstacle in Iron Keep.

bosses.name: Old Iron King
bosses.lore: The ancient ruler who built Iron Keep over a lake of lava, consumed by pride and pulled into the magma by his own sins.

bosses.name: Executioner's Chariot
bosses.lore: A skeletal horse pulling a bladed chariot that runs a loop through a narrow bridge, forcing the player to pull a lever to stop it.

bosses.name: Prowling Magus and Congregation
bosses.lore: A corrupt magus surrounded by a throng of hollow faithful who heal him endlessly unless cleared away first.

bosses.name: The Duke's Dear Freja
bosses.lore: A colossal spider with a malformed head growing on its back, a creation of Navlaan or perhaps Aldia, infesting the spider-ridden cove.

bosses.name: The Royal Rat Vanguard
bosses.lore: A massive rat commanding an endless swarm of smaller rats — only by killing the Vanguard itself can the assault be ended.

bosses.name: The Royal Rat Authority
bosses.lore: A giant rat with elongated limbs and a magical presence, commanding its smaller kin and wielding dark breath as a weapon.

bosses.name: Scorpioness Najka
bosses.lore: A woman transformed into a scorpion by the scholars of Aldia, she burrows underground and launches sand attacks from below.

bosses.name: Demon of Song
bosses.lore: A toad-like creature that hides its true form behind a beautiful illusion, luring prey with the appearance of a young woman.

bosses.name: Velstadt, the Royal Aegis
bosses.lore: Vendrick's most loyal knight, who followed his king into the Undead Crypt and guards his tomb with fanatical devotion.

bosses.name: Looking Glass Knight
bosses.lore: A colossal knight wielding a mirrored shield that can summon invaders from other worlds as living weapons to fight alongside him.

bosses.name: Vendrick
bosses.lore: The hollow king of Drangleic, stripped of his soul by Nashandra, who wanders his crypt in a cursed stupor still wearing his crown.

bosses.name: Guardian Dragon
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon chained above a bonfire in Aldia's laboratories, a subject of the scholar's research into the nature of dragons.

bosses.name: Ancient Dragon
bosses.lore: A colossal dragon of legend atop the Dragon Shrine, whose knowledge of the curse and its history surpasses even the gods.

bosses.name: Throne Watcher and Throne Defender
bosses.lore: Two knights who rose to guard the throne of want — they revive each other until both are defeated simultaneously.

bosses.name: Nashandra
bosses.lore: The queen of Drangleic and a fragment of Manus, who manipulated Vendrick and the Bearer of the Curse to claim the First Flame.

bosses.name: Aldia, Scholar of the First Sin
bosses.lore: Vendrick's elder brother who sought to break the undead curse through extreme research, transforming himself into something beyond human.

bosses.name: Elana, the Squalid Queen
bosses.lore: A Daughter of Chaos who consumed the Dark of countless souls, she can summon the undead and even resurrect Velstadt to her aid.

bosses.name: Sinh, the Slumbering Dragon
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon whose breath is a virulent poison, sleeping in the depths of Sanctum City and considered sacred by its inhabitants.

bosses.name: Afflicted Graverobber, Ancient Soldier Varg, and Cerah the Old Explorer
bosses.lore: Three former Dragon Remnants who lost faith in their cause and turned to robbery, fought as a challenging three-man gauntlet.

bosses.name: Fume Knight
bosses.lore: Raime, a former captain of Drangleic who was exiled after a dispute with Velstadt, wielding an ultra-greatsword and a flame-imbued curved sword.

bosses.name: Sir Alonne
bosses.lore: The Old Iron King's most loyal knight and the inspiration for the Alonne Knights — a master swordsman from the east who serves even in death.

bosses.name: Blue Smelter Demon
bosses.lore: A variant of the Smelter Demon infused with a blue-white magical fire instead of red flame, requiring a gruelling gauntlet to reach.

bosses.name: Aava, the King's Pet
bosses.lore: The Ivory King's beloved feline companion, invisible until the Imperfect are defeated. She guards the entrance to the frozen city with ferocity.

bosses.name: Burnt Ivory King
bosses.lore: The Ivory King who sacrificed himself to seal away a chaos flame, summoned back from his act of heroism to face the player one final time.

bosses.name: Lud and Zallen, the King's Pets
bosses.lore: A pair of enormous feline guardians similar to Aava, fought sequentially in a blizzard after one of the game's most punishing approach routes.

bosses.name: Darklurker
bosses.lore: A mysterious angelic being dwelling at the base of the Dark Chasm — one of DS2's hardest optional bosses, splitting into two mid-fight.

bosses.name: Cleric Beast
bosses.lore: A clergyman transformed into a towering beast by the scourge of beasts — the first true horror the Hunter encounters.

bosses.name: Father Gascoigne
bosses.lore: A hunter who succumbed to the scourge mid-hunt. His music box, a gift from his daughter, is the only thing that slows him.

bosses.name: Blood-Starved Beast
bosses.lore: A beast maddened by the old blood, naked and frenzied, haunting the ruins of the forbidden district of Yharnam.

bosses.name: Vicar Amelia
bosses.lore: The head Vicar of the Healing Church, transformed mid-prayer into a massive golden beast clutching a holy pendant.

bosses.name: Shadow of Yharnam
bosses.lore: Three shadow figures who act as guardians of Byrgenwerth, wielding katanas, candles, and serpent sorcery in concert.

bosses.name: Darkbeast Paarl
bosses.lore: An ancient beast crackling with lightning, so old that its flesh has rotted away leaving only a skeleton of electricity.

bosses.name: The Witch of Hemwick
bosses.lore: Twin witches who summon Mad Ones to swarm and distract the Hunter while they remain invisible.

bosses.name: Rom, the Vacuous Spider
bosses.lore: A former student of Byrgenwerth, transformed into a spider. Killing her lifts the veil hiding the Great Ones' presence.

bosses.name: The One Reborn
bosses.lore: A horrific congregation of corpses fused into one massive body, reborn again and again through ritual incantation.

bosses.name: Martyr Logarius
bosses.lore: A scholar-turned-executioner who sacrificed himself to seal Cainhurst Castle, his spectral robes now serving as a prison.

bosses.name: Celestial Emissary
bosses.lore: A cluster of small celestial beings that merge into one enormous alien creature, a failed experiment of the Healing Church.

bosses.name: Ebrietas, Daughter of the Cosmos
bosses.lore: A Great One left behind by her kin and adopted by the Healing Church, weeping amid the cosmos she can no longer reach.

bosses.name: Micolash, Host of the Nightmare
bosses.lore: A scholar who willingly trapped himself in the Hunter's Nightmare to commune with Kos through arcane rituals.

bosses.name: Amygdala
bosses.lore: A towering Great One with long limbs and a face of eyes, lurking at the centre of the Nightmare Frontier.

bosses.name: Mergo's Wet Nurse
bosses.lore: A mysterious Great One who tends to Mergo, a child of the Pthumerian queen. It wields multiple spectral blades.

bosses.name: Gehrman, the First Hunter
bosses.lore: The founder of the workshop and master of all hunters, who serves the Moon Presence and guards the Hunter's Dream.

bosses.name: Moon Presence
bosses.lore: A Great One who formed a contract with Gehrman, feeding on human insight and trapping hunters in the dream.

bosses.name: Ludwig, the Holy Blade
bosses.lore: The first hunter of the Healing Church, transformed into a monstrous steed, who reclaims his humanity mid-fight.

bosses.name: Living Failures
bosses.lore: Failed experiments of the Healing Church who sought to become Great Ones, now hurling arcane projectiles in desperate unison.

bosses.name: Lady Maria of the Astral Clocktower
bosses.lore: A descendant of Queen Yharnam and the finest student of Gehrman, who sealed herself away out of guilt.

bosses.name: Orphan of Kos
bosses.lore: Born from the corpse of the Great One Kos, this infant wields its own placenta as a weapon and screams with impossible grief.

bosses.name: Laurence, the First Vicar
bosses.lore: The founder of the Healing Church and architect of the blood ministration, now trapped in the Nightmare as a burning beast.

bosses.name: Beast-possessed Soul
bosses.lore: A soul consumed by the beast scourge, found stalking the depths of the Pthumerian labyrinths.

bosses.name: Bloodletting Beast
bosses.lore: A massive beast with a severed head that continues to fight, its exposed ribcage housing a parasitic worm.

bosses.name: Keeper of the Old Lords
bosses.lore: An ancient guardian of the Pthumerian rituals, wielding fire and arcane power deep within the labyrinths.

bosses.name: Merciless Watcher
bosses.lore: A gang of Watchers that attack together — one wielding a club, one a gun, one a molotov — lurking in the old labyrinths.

bosses.name: Pthumerian Descendant
bosses.lore: A swift and silent Pthumerian warrior who dances through combat with dual blades and venomous strikes.

bosses.name: Pthumerian Elder
bosses.lore: A towering ancient Pthumerian sorcerer who commands arcane energy and space itself to repel intruders.

bosses.name: Undead Giant
bosses.lore: A massive undead creature chained at the waist, dragging its bonds as weapons through the Pthumerian depths.

bosses.name: Watch Dog of the Old Lords
bosses.lore: A colossal burning beast that served the old Pthumerian lords as a guardian of their most sacred chalice rituals.

bosses.name: Yharnam, Pthumerian Queen
bosses.lore: The queen of ancient Pthumeria, whose blood was used in the Healing Church rituals. She appears in visions, carrying a stillborn child.

bosses.name: Abhorrent Beast
bosses.lore: A fully transformed beast crackling with lightning, representing the most advanced stage of the scourge in the Loran labyrinths.

bosses.name: Loran Darkbeast
bosses.lore: A skeletal beast of pure lightning native to the fallen city of Loran, even more powerful than Darkbeast Paarl.

bosses.name: Loran Silverbeast
bosses.lore: A white-furred beast from the ruins of Loran that spits fire and charges with reckless ferocity.

bosses.name: Forgotten Madman
bosses.lore: A hunter driven to madness by the labyrinth, who calls for a phantom ally and fights with frenzied desperation.

bosses.name: Brainsucker
bosses.lore: A humanoid creature with a parasite for a head that drains insight from hunters, diminishing their grip on reality.

bosses.name: Maneater Boar
bosses.lore: An enormous rabid boar that charges and pins hunters in the enclosed corridors of the Pthumerian labyrinths.

bosses.name: Gyoubu Masataka Oniwa
bosses.lore: A fearsome general on horseback who charges with his enormous spear, roaring war cries across the battlefield.

bosses.name: Genichiro Ashina
bosses.lore: The grandson of the sword saint, desperately fighting to save Ashina from collapse — by any means necessary.

bosses.name: Folding Screen Monkeys
bosses.lore: Four divine monkeys that must be caught through stealth in an illusory realm — hear no evil, see no evil, speak no evil, and death.

bosses.name: Guardian Ape
bosses.lore: A massive ape protecting a Lotus of the Palace. Even beheading it is not enough — it rises again headless, wielding its own skull.

bosses.name: Headless Ape
bosses.lore: The Guardian Ape reunited with its mate, fighting as a pair in one of the game's most chaotic duo encounters.

bosses.name: Corrupted Monk (Illusion)
bosses.lore: The illusory first phase of the Corrupted Monk, fought amid the mist near Mibu Village. Defeating the phantom reveals the deception was just the beginning.

bosses.name: Sword Saint Isshin
bosses.lore: The true final boss: Genichiro's ultimate gambit summons the young Isshin at the peak of his power, wielding spear, sword, and lightning.

bosses.name: Corrupted Monk (True Form)
bosses.lore: The true form of the Corrupted Monk atop the Fountainhead Palace bridge — a three-phase battle demanding endurance and precise mikiri counters.

bosses.name: Divine Dragon
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon from the Divine Realm, the source of the undying power the Dragon's Heritage draws from.

bosses.name: Great Shinobi Owl
bosses.lore: Wolf's adoptive father and the master shinobi who trained him — a ruthless pragmatist who puts mission above all.

bosses.name: Lady Butterfly
bosses.lore: An aged shinobi master who trained Wolf as a child, now testing him with illusions and phantom butterflies.

bosses.name: Demon of Hatred
bosses.lore: The lingering hatred of an Ashina general transformed by Shura's fire — a massive demon with sweeping fire attacks.

bosses.name: Isshin Ashina (Shura Ending)
bosses.lore: The aging lord of Ashina fought at the climax of the Shura path — Wolf, consumed by Shura, must slay both Emma and the old lord before descending into darkness.

bosses.name: Great Shinobi Owl (Father)
bosses.lore: A second encounter with Owl in the Hirata Estate memory, unlocked in the Purification ending. More aggressive and armed with new prosthetic tools.

bosses.name: Inner Genichiro
bosses.lore: The inner reflection of Genichiro in the Gauntlet of Strength — a more unforgiving version of the castle rooftop duel with no room for error.

bosses.name: Inner Isshin
bosses.lore: The inner reflection of Sword Saint Isshin in the Gauntlet of Strength — considered by many to be the single hardest challenge in all of Sekiro.

bosses.name: Inner Owl
bosses.lore: The inner reflection of Great Shinobi Owl fought in the Gauntlet, elevated in speed and aggression beyond either encounter in the main game.

bosses.name: Chained Ogre (Stairway)
bosses.lore: A chained ogre of tremendous strength blocking the ascent to Ashina. His instant-kill grapples punish the reckless, but fire makes him flinch.

bosses.name: Chained Ogre (Antechamber)
bosses.lore: A second chained ogre lurking inside the castle antechamber — more aggressive in the enclosed stone corridor than his counterpart on the hillside.

bosses.name: Shichimen Warrior (Abandoned Dungeon)
bosses.lore: A spectral warrior that fires beams of spirit energy and inflicts Terror — one of the first encounters requiring Divine Confetti and careful resource management.

bosses.name: Shichimen Warrior (Ashina Depths)
bosses.lore: A Shichimen Warrior lurking in the depths of Ashina, more powerful than the dungeon variant and appearing in a cramped space.

bosses.name: Shichimen Warrior (Fountainhead Palace)
bosses.lore: The most powerful Shichimen Warrior, fought underwater near Fountainhead Palace, with an extended Terror aura and relentless spirit beams.

bosses.name: Juzou the Drunkard
bosses.lore: An obese, sake-addled warrior surrounded by lackeys. His poison breath and erratic swings make him a memorable early-game challenge.

bosses.name: Juzou the Drunkard (Returns)
bosses.lore: Juzou reappears in the second Hirata Estate memory during the Purification ending — the same drunken fighter, revisited with all a veteran's skills.

bosses.name: Tokujiro the Glutton
bosses.lore: A drunkard miniboss similar to Juzou hiding in the hidden forest, his sake breath and heavy strikes making him a dangerous ambush.

bosses.name: Lone Shadow Longswordsman
bosses.lore: A shadow warrior lurking in the well near the reservoir, skilled in unpredictable kick combos and longsword pressure.

bosses.name: Lone Shadow Vilehand
bosses.lore: A Lone Shadow who infiltrates the Ashina Dojo after the first invasion, mixing poison and lightning-fast claw strikes.

bosses.name: Lone Shadow Masanaga the Spear-Bearer
bosses.lore: A spear-wielding shadow warrior who appears at the Great Serpent Shrine after Great Shinobi Owl is defeated.

bosses.name: Lone Shadow (Hirata Memory 2)
bosses.lore: A Lone Shadow stationed in the second Hirata Estate memory, guarding the path for players pursuing the Purification ending.

bosses.name: Jinsuke Saze
bosses.lore: An Ashina Elite swordsman whose horizontal blade sweeps are nearly invisible — he demands the most precise deflect timing in the entire game.

bosses.name: Ujinari Mizuo
bosses.lore: A red-eyed Ashina Elite who appears in the second invasion, his desperation and crimson gaze making him faster and more fearless than Jinsuke Saze.

bosses.name: Long-arm Centipede Sen-un
bosses.lore: A grotesque centipede-limbed creature whose blurring flurry of arms overwhelms players who try to deflect everything — letting them pass is the secret.

bosses.name: Long-arm Centipede Giraffe
bosses.lore: The long-necked centipede fighter at the Gun Fort, requiring the same counter-intuitive patience as Sen-un — wait for the opening, then punish.

bosses.name: Snake Eyes Shirufuji
bosses.lore: A marksman of the snake-eyes elite stationed at the Gun Fort, combining devastating rifle shots with lightning-quick melee strikes.

bosses.name: Snake Eyes Shirahagi
bosses.lore: A snake-eyed warrior lurking in the poison pool of Ashina Depths, ambushing with gunfire and brutal close combat from the fog.

bosses.name: Blazing Bull
bosses.lore: An enormous bull set ablaze and unleashed as a siege weapon — it charges endlessly and is nearly impossible to deflect. Sprint, sidestep, and close in.

bosses.name: Sakura Bull of the Palace
bosses.lore: A divine bull wreathed in sakura petals that guards the Fountainhead Palace, its charges cloaked in beautiful but deadly blossoms.

bosses.name: Seven Ashina Spears - Shikibu Toshikatsu Yamauchi
bosses.lore: A spear master of the Seven Ashina Spears stationed at the Moon Tower, his long-reach thrust combos and wide sweeps punishing impatient players.

bosses.name: Seven Ashina Spears - Shume Masaji Oniwa
bosses.lore: The last of the Seven Spears encountered at the Ashina Reservoir in the late game, fighting with desperate fury to defend the crumbling castle.

bosses.name: General Naomori Kawarada
bosses.lore: The first named general encountered in Sekiro — a tutorial-adjacent miniboss that introduces the deflect and posture system in a fair fight.

bosses.name: General Tenzen Yamauchi
bosses.lore: An Ashina general stationed beyond the stairways, a step up from Kawarada with more aggressive follow-up attacks.

bosses.name: General Kuranosuke Matsumoto
bosses.lore: An Ashina general fighting on the castle stairways with broad sweeping attacks and resilient posture.

bosses.name: Leader Shigenori Yamauchi
bosses.lore: A general who serves as a de-facto tutorial boss in the early outskirts, teaching thrust counters before the game opens up.

bosses.name: Headless (Ashina Outskirts)
bosses.lore: An ancient headless warrior spawned from the earth, inflicting Terror and Curse — requires Divine Confetti to damage and Pacifying Agent to cure.

bosses.name: Headless (Sunken Valley)
bosses.lore: A headless spirit lurking in the caves of the Sunken Valley, its Terror aura and phantom strikes demanding careful resource management.

bosses.name: Headless (Ashina Depths)
bosses.lore: A headless warrior in the depths of Ashina — one of the more punishing encounters due to the narrow terrain that limits movement.

bosses.name: Headless (Ashina Castle Underwater)
bosses.lore: An underwater headless spirit beneath Ashina Castle, its movements distorted by the water and its Terror radius spreading in every direction.

bosses.name: Headless (Fountainhead Palace Underwater)
bosses.lore: The final headless warrior lurking in the sacred waters beneath Fountainhead Palace, rewarding the player with the Withered Red Gourd upon defeat.

bosses.name: Shinobi Hunter Enshin of Misen
bosses.lore: A spear-wielding shinobi hunter in the Hirata Estate who excels at sweeping attacks with deceptive range — jumping and countering is key.

bosses.name: Shigekichi of the Red Guard
bosses.lore: A fire-wielding Red Guard who appears on the castle grounds after the second invasion, bringing oil flasks and flame attacks to the battle.

bosses.name: Mist Noble
bosses.lore: A serene noble whose flute generates the deadly mist of the hidden forest — the most unusual miniboss in Sekiro, defeated simply by reaching him.

bosses.name: O'Rin of the Water
bosses.lore: A water spirit drifting along the river of Mibu Village in the guise of a woman, attacking with spectral tendrils when approached without the correct prayer.

bosses.name: Okami Leader Shizu
bosses.lore: The lightning-wreathed leader of the Okami warrior women, channeling divine thunder at the base of the great Sakura tree in Fountainhead Palace.

bosses.name: Armored Warrior
bosses.lore: A fully armored knight on a narrow bridge in Senpou Temple — he cannot be killed by conventional means. The only path is to knock him off the bridge.
